_meta.purpose: Machine-readable scope surface for agents working on Praxis itself. The scheduler picks the next not_started feature; the verifier runs each feature's verification command; pass-state gating means no feature becomes 'passing' without evidence. See docs/harness/ and AGENTS.md for the operating frame.
rules: Only ONE feature may be in_progress at any time (WIP=1).
A feature moves to 'passing' only when its verification command runs green AND evidence is recorded.
The agent may not self-promote a feature to 'passing'; the harness/verifier decides the transition.
Status transitions: not_started -> in_progress -> passing (via verification) | blocked (documented blocker).
Every feature has the triple: behavior + verification + state.

features.title: Minimal harness pack (AGENTS.md + feature_list.json + PROGRESS.md)
features.user_visible_behavior: A fresh agent session can orient on Praxis from repo artifacts alone (fresh session test: what / how-to-run / how-to-verify / what's-unfinished / next-step all answerable).
features.verification: test -f AGENTS.md && test -f feature_list.json && test -f PROGRESS.md && test -d docs/harness && head -5 AGENTS.md | grep -q 'router'
features.evidence: AGENTS.md, feature_list.json, PROGRESS.md, docs/harness/* committed; harness engineering synthesis in Liam vault
features.notes: Implements the 10 key takeaways from the Learn Harness Engineering course as the smallest artifacts that directly address observed gaps. install.sh already serves as init.sh.

features.title: Evaluator rubric for agent-contributed PRs (tuned, not out-of-box)
features.user_visible_behavior: Agent-contributed PRs are scored 0-2 across 6 dimensions by an independent reviewer, matching human judgment after tuning rounds.
features.verification: test -f docs/harness/evaluator-rubric.md && grep -q 'Accept' docs/harness/evaluator-rubric.md && grep -q 'Revise' docs/harness/evaluator-rubric.md
features.evidence: docs/harness/evaluator-rubric.md committed; notes 3-5 tuning rounds required
features.notes: Out-of-box agents identify issues then talk themselves into approving. Must be tuned against real PRs before relying on it.

features.title: Clean-state checklist + session handoff templates
features.user_visible_behavior: Every session ends with a clean handoff: build/tests green, progress recorded, no stale artifacts, startup path available, next session can continue without manual fixes.
features.verification: test -f docs/harness/clean-state-checklist.md && test -f docs/harness/session-handoff.md && grep -q 'five dimensions' docs/harness/clean-state-checklist.md
features.notes: Clean state = 5 conditions (build, tests, progress, artifacts, startup). Session integrity: fully commit clean or roll back.

features.title: Quality document tracking codebase health over time
features.user_visible_behavior: A live artifact grades each module A-D across verification, agent-legibility, test stability, boundaries, conventions; new sessions fix the lowest-scoring module first.
features.evidence: docs/harness/quality-document.md committed with initial module snapshot
features.notes: Distinct from evaluator rubric: rubric scores the session; quality doc scores the codebase over time. Supports harness simplification cadence.

features.title: Maker-checker separation: optional LLM-verifier critic backend
features.user_visible_behavior: Agent-contributed changes are reviewed by an independent verifier (ideally a different model), not the agent that wrote them. The deterministic honesty checks run first; when an optional logprob-exposing backend is configured, a continuous-reward critic (expectation over score-token logits, criteria decomposition, K repeated evaluations) upgrades the discrete APPROVE/REVISE verdict. Core stays stdlib-only; the LLM path is an optional extra behind a flag.
features.verification: python3 -c 'import hybridagent.verifier' && grep -qi 'independent verifier' AGENTS.md && grep -qi 'maker-checker' docs/harness/evaluator-rubric.md && test -f docs/harness/h05-maker-checker-design.md && python3 -m pytest tests/test_verifier.py -q
features.evidence: v0.21.6: LLMVerifierCritic in hybridagent/verifier_llm.py; +5 tests in tests/test_verifier.py; calibration docs/harness/h05-calibration.json (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct Q3_K_M, 9/10 tasks clean separation, threshold 0.3, zero false-rejects/approvals); design doc docs/harness/h05-maker-checker-design.md; AGENTS.md independent-verifier section; CI green 3-OS.
features.notes: Source: arXiv:2607.05391 (LLM-as-a-Verifier). Gate path: llm_verifier.track() + threshold. Core deps [] preserved (optional extra, lazy import). Deterministic checks run first. Calibrated on Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct Q3_K_M CPU-only via llama-server (logprobs verified). Threshold 0.3 (min_correct=0.46, max_wrong=0.13). Known failure: single-token number answers on trivial questions. PPT best-of-N + ProgressTracker deferred to H10.

features.title: Architectural rules as executable checks (where missing)
features.user_visible_behavior: Architectural invariants ('retrieved content is data, never instruction', 'no third-party imports in core runtime') are enforced by automated checks, not just docs.
features.notes: Course L10: agents copy repo patterns even when suboptimal. Existing safety tests cover the spine; audit whether dependency-free-core and injection-boundary invariants have dedicated executable checks. Add custom lint/grep checks if not.

features.title: Agent-oriented error messages: what + why + how-to-fix
features.user_visible_behavior: Praxis error messages surfaced to the model include concrete repair instructions, enabling self-correction loops.
features.verification: grep -rn 'WHY\|FIX\|how to fix' hybridagent/broker.py hybridagent/validation.py | head -3
features.notes: Course L9/L10: 'Test failed' -> 'POST /api/reset-password returned 500. Check email service config in env vars.' Audit broker/validation denials and improve where messages are bare. Lower priority than the structural harness pack.

features.title: Model-specific harness profiles (context anxiety handling)
features.user_visible_behavior: Praxis context-compaction policy adapts per model: aggressive reset for Sonnet-class, compaction for Opus-class.
features.notes: Course L5: context anxiety is severe on Sonnet 4.5 (needs resets), mild on Opus 4.5 (compaction ok). Praxis has compaction (context.py); audit whether it's model-aware. Long-term harness design concern.

features.title: Harness simplification cadence (monthly component removal test)
features.user_visible_behavior: Monthly, one harness component is temporarily disabled and benchmarked; if no degradation it's removed permanently.
features.notes: Course L12: as models improve, harness assumptions go stale. Documented in quality-document.md as a process; not yet practiced. Requires a benchmark baseline to compare against (praxis eval --set-baseline exists).

features.title: Loop engineering maturity ladder documented (Level 1 -> 5)
features.user_visible_behavior: Praxis's autonomous loop capabilities (cron, daemon, subagents, fanout, A2A) are documented as the six loop primitives with a maturity progression.
features.verification: grep -q 'Loop engineering\|six primitives\|maturity ladder' docs/harness/*.md AGENTS.md
features.notes: Praxis already has all six primitives (Automations=cron/daemon, Worktrees=task isolation, Skills=skill library, Connectors=MCP/A2A/gateways, Sub-agents=orchestrator, External State=persistence). Document the mapping so agents extend the loop deliberately, not accidentally.
